ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for stirred-fried pork vermicelli; mapped to generic pork dish class as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (pork meat dish) used as closest defensible category; no specific stir-fried pork vermicelli code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single serving of stirred-fried pork vermicelli is approximately 250 g, equivalent to one plate as typically served at a hawker stall or casual restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Stirred-Fried Pork Vermicelli — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Stirred-Fried Pork Vermicelli (cr_id G00010603). All per-100g values preserved from the canonical HPB record. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork stir-fry analogue (USDA SR Legacy #17062) as approximately 3.5g/100g. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of glass noodles (mung bean vermicelli) — University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate; carbohydrate_g reported as 0.0 per HPB canonical source for this entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: The HPB canonical source reports carbohydrate_g as 0.0g/100g for this specific dish entry, which reflects the pork-dominant analytical sample. Because carbohydrates are reported as negligible for this HPB record, glycemic load per serving is 0.0. A numeric GI of 35 is assigned as an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for glass noodles / mung bean vermicelli (GI range 26–39, low category), representing the noodle component of the dish should the full dish including vermicelli be consumed. This analogous GI is provided for completeness and clinical reference; the effective glycemic load for this HPB-defined entry remains 0.0 due to the 0g reported carbohydrate.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; potassium is very low at 47 mg/100g providing no meaningful offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; a standard 250g serving delivers approximately 2625 mg sodium, exceeding the entire daily recommended limit of 2000 mg, and the negligible potassium content (118 mg per serving) provides no compensatory benefit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate reported as 0g/100g per HPB source, but dish name includes vermicelli which typically contains starch; high sodium at 1050 mg/100g may worsen insulin resistance over time
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the HPB-reported carbohydrate content is 0g/100g suggesting minimal acute glycemic impact, individuals with type 2 diabetes should verify the actual noodle content with the vendor, monitor portion size carefully, and be aware that the very high sodium load (1050 mg/100g) may contribute to insulin resistance and cardiovascular risk over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium 1050 mg/100g, cholesterol 89 mg/100g, fat 10.5 g/100g including estimated saturated fat 3.5 g/100g; pork is a source of arachidonic acid
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish poses a high cardiovascular risk due to extreme sodium content (1050 mg/100g), moderate fat with saturated fat contribution, and cholesterol at 89 mg/100g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption and request reduced-sodium preparation with smaller portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is high in arachidonic acid (omega-6) amplifying inflammatory pathways; sodium at 1050 mg/100g far exceeds the 400 mg/100g RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish due to the pro-inflammatory combination of pork (arachidonic acid source) and extreme sodium; substituting chicken or tofu and requesting reduced seasoning are the primary mitigation strategies.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stirred-Fried Pork Vermicelli (Burmese: ဝက်ကြေးအိုးဆီချက်, romanized: Wak Kye Ar Oo Si Chet) is a Burmese-origin stir-fried dish consisting of sliced or minced pork cooked with glass noodles (vermicelli) in a wok over high heat, seasoned with soy sauce, fish sauce, garlic, sesame oil, and chili. The dish is characterized by a savory, umami-rich flavor profile with a slightly oily texture from the wok-frying technique. It is commonly served as a main course at lunch or dinner in hawker-style settings in Singapore and Myanmar. Per 100g, the HPB Singapore database reports 171 kcal, 19.1g protein, 10.5g fat, 0g carbohydrate, and 1050mg sodium. The very high sodium content (1050 mg/100g) makes this dish unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The high protein content (19.1g/100g) supports satiety and muscle maintenance. The reported 0g carbohydrate is notable given the vermicelli component and likely reflects the pork-dominant fraction of the HPB analytical sample; consumers should verify actual noodle content. Cholesterol is 89 mg/100g. This dish is not halal due to pork content and contains soy and fish allergens.

Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy pork stir-fry analogue and documented. GI assigned as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database glass noodle entry. All adaptations clearly noted.
notes: carbohydrate_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this specific entry; this likely reflects the pork-dominant analytical sample rather than the full dish including vermicelli noodles — confirmed zero per HPB source for this record, but consumers should verify actual noodle content with vendor. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported by HPB for this entry; not available in HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient data beyond the HPB-reported fields not available in HPB Foodo database for this entry as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g estimated at 3.5g/100g from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pork stir-fry analogue (#17062) as HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry. glycemic_index set to 35 (low category) as an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database glass noodle entry (GI range 26–39); glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.0 per HPB canonical source for this entry. The dish is not halal due to pork content. Burmese-origin dish served in Singapore context; multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
